title: eremophila citrina
intro: Eremophila citrina, also known as the Lemon-scented Emu Bush, is a flowering shrub native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eremophila citrina include its ability to relieve congestion, calm the nervous system, and support immune function. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
